description: Detects when a security group ingress rule is added that allows traffic from 0.0.0.0/0 (the entire internet). While ports 80 and 443 may be intentionally public-facing, opening other ports (SSH, RDP, databases) to the world is a critical misconfiguration that attackers exploit for initial access or persistence.
query_kql: eventName:"AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress" AND request_ip_permissions:*0.0.0.0* AND NOT errorCode:*
triage_steps: Identify which security group was modified (requestParameters.groupId)
If ports 80/443 — may be intentional for web servers (lower priority)
If ports 22/3389/3306/5432 — CRITICAL, should never be open to the world
Identify all EC2 instances associated with this security group
Check for immediate connection attempts from external IPs
false_positive_notes: Web servers and load balancers legitimately need 80/443 open to the internet. DevOps may temporarily open SSH for debugging (bad practice but common). Terraform/CloudFormation deployments may create public-facing rules as designed.
tuning_guidance: Consider splitting into two rules: HIGH for non-80/443 ports, MEDIUM for 80/443. Exclude known IaC service roles. In strict environments, alert on ALL 0.0.0.0/0 rules regardless of port.
response_actions: Remove the 0.0.0.0/0 rule immediately for non-web ports
Replace with the specific IP range that needs access
Investigate who made the change and why
Check VPC Flow Logs for inbound connections while the rule was active
Consider implementing AWS Config rules to auto-remediate open security groups
